skill.name: codexemailskill
skill.description: Guides AI agents through repository-aware email template, content, QA, and rollout work with approval gates for live email systems.
skill.summary: Plan, implement, and verify repo-aware email code changes with clear approval gates.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed all five static findings as false positives in descriptive skill text. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network behavior, scripts, or executable code were found in the reviewed files. The skill remains low risk because it guides repository reading and scoped email edits.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match
low_findings.description: The static matches occur in normal descriptive text, including agent names and live-system risk language. No cryptographic function, hash implementation, encryption setting, or credential handling is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain only YAML or Markdown guidance, and the matched lines do not define or call any cryptographic algorithm. Confidence is very low for a real crypto issue because there is no executable crypto context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: The static matches refer to locating email surfaces and making scoped code changes inside a repository. This is normal context gathering for an implementation skill, not host reconnaissance or inventory collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding procedure limits work to repository email files and verification steps. Confidence is very low for malicious reconnaissance because there are no commands, scripts, or instructions to enumerate the host system.

content.value_statement: Email implementation often spans templates, content, tests, and approval steps. This skill helps AI agents make scoped repository changes with clear QA checks and rollout boundaries.
actual_capabilities: Identifies email templates, components, content files, preview routes, tests, and export scripts in a repository.
Plans scoped email changes that match existing naming, styling, localization, and data-loading patterns.
Guides implementation of copy updates, layout fixes, template modules, data fields, and tracking parameters.
Recommends focused verification with render checks, type checks, snapshots, lint, unit tests, or preview builds.
Separates code changes from live sends, contact imports, DNS changes, suppression edits, and production automations.
limitations: It does not send live email or approve production rollout actions.
It depends on the repository having accessible email files, previews, or tests.
It cannot guarantee rendering across every email client without external preview tooling.
It provides workflow guidance, not an ESP-specific migration engine.

use_cases.title: Fix a Template Regression
use_cases.description: Update a React Email or component template while preserving local patterns and verifying the rendered result.

use_cases.title: Prepare Campaign Content Changes
use_cases.description: Change MDX or content-system email copy with clear evidence, tracking checks, and production approval boundaries.

use_cases.title: Review Email System Risk
use_cases.description: Audit proposed email code changes for scope, verification, rollout risk, and live-system approval requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Email Surface
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to identify the email templates, content files, preview routes, and tests related to this request.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Scoped Email Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a repo-aware plan for updating this email template while preserving existing patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Implement and Verify a Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to make the smallest coherent email code change and run the narrowest useful verification.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Production Email Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review this email change for rollout risk, approval gates, tracking behavior, and remaining verification gaps.

output_examples.input: Update the password reset email so the button copy matches the new product language.
output_examples.output: A concise change plan, the affected template path, the copy update, suggested render verification, and a rollout note requiring approval before any send.

output_examples.input: Review this welcome-series email change before it is imported into the ESP.
output_examples.output: A review summary covering changed files, dynamic fields, tracking parameters, rendering risk, completed checks, and production steps that need approval.

output_examples.input: Add a fallback for a missing customer first name in an email template.
output_examples.output: A scoped implementation approach, fallback behavior, relevant tests or previews, and a short report of remaining risk.

best_practices: Read adjacent email files before editing so local structure and naming stay consistent.
Keep production actions separate from repository changes and require explicit approval for live-system operations.
Run the narrowest verification that proves the user-visible email behavior changed correctly.
anti_patterns: Do not create a parallel email framework when the repository already has established templates.
Do not change DNS, suppressions, imports, sends, or automations as part of a code-only request.
Do not skip fallback handling for dynamic fields that may be empty or undefined.

faq.question: Can this skill send emails?
faq.answer: No. It separates repository work from live sends and requires explicit approval for production email actions.

faq.question: Does it support React Email?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide React Email edits when the repository includes those components or related previews.

faq.question: Can it edit MDX email content?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help update MDX content while checking nearby patterns, localization, and verification paths.

faq.question: What verification does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends focused checks such as render previews, type checks, lint, snapshots, unit tests, or visual smoke checks.

faq.question: Is it tied to one email service provider?
faq.answer: No. The workflow is repository-focused and treats ESP imports or migrations as separate approval steps.

faq.question: What makes this useful for AI coding agents?
faq.answer: It gives agents a clear process for scoped email code changes, evidence-based reports, and production risk boundaries.
